summary: DHH shares a News.com article that bills Ruby on Rails as 'High Impact' and notes the framework's growing mainstream recognition. He acknowledges the article is somewhat ego-stroking but appreciates how the journalist captured his excitement. He points to Sun CEO Jonathan Schwartz linking to Rails as further evidence of mainstream adoption, and teases the upcoming Rails 1.0 release as the next milestone.
key_points: News.com featured Ruby on Rails as 'High Impact,' comparing its trajectory to Linux's early days
Sun Microsystems CEO Jonathan Schwartz publicly acknowledged Rails, signaling enterprise-level awareness
Rails was approaching its 1.0 release, which DHH positioned as the next inflection point for adoption
money_quotes: So it's a little bit more of an ego-wank than what might have been desired, but I can cope.
This is exactly how things like Linux got started
Ruby on Rails is certainly making its way into the mainstream.
Now to take it all up a notch with the bang of 1.0.
key_insight: By late 2005, Ruby on Rails had crossed from niche developer tool to mainstream tech press coverage and enterprise CEO attention, validating its rapid rise ahead of the 1.0 launch.
